title: Pinalia hutchinsoniana
taxonomy: Pinalia hutchinsoniana is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places Pinalia hutchinsoniana among the approximately 30,000 to 40,000 known species of orchids worldwide. Further classification within the family Orchidaceae places Pinalia hutchinsoniana in the subfamily Orchidoideae. The genus Pinalia is a subgenus within the larger genus Coelogyne, which is also part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. Pinalia hutchinsoniana is a member of the kingdom Plantae and is further classified within the phylum Angiospermophyta.
uses: Pinalia hutchinsoniana, a species of orchid, has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on the human body. In some cultures, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is sometimes used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Further studies are necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.

Its leaves and rhizomes are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
